codeReview.summary: This revision adds direct DashboardQuickSwitchView inspection coverage, but it still misses required assertion quality for actionable control wiring: Retry presence/wiring and accessibility-label checks are not actually validated by the named tests.

issues.description: Retry control presence is not directly asserted. `test_errorState_hasRetryButton` uses `sut.find(ViewType.Button.self)` (line 1331), which can succeed with the header Settings button even if the error-state Retry control is removed.

Retry action wiring is not verified. `test_errorState_retryButtonTriggersReload` taps `buttons.first` (line 1571) from `findAll(Button)` and does not assert post-tap state change, so it can pass while tapping the header Settings button or while Retry is non-functional.

Accessibility-label tests are misnamed relative to assertions: `test_errorState_retryButton_hasAccessibilityLabel` and `test_errorState_openSettingsButton_hasAccessibilityLabel` never assert the configured accessibility labels; they only assert generic button counts and text presence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker marked `followedProcedure: true`, but the skill's required broad app test and manual UI sanity step were not evidenced in handoff verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: switcher-ui-worker SKILL.md step 6 requires scoped tests plus `scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh`, and step 7 requires manual UI sanity checks; handoff `2026-04-09T22-34-11-929Z__fast-surfaces-add-dashboard-error-ui-render-tests__d0963f83-3870-414c-96d8-885ccfb0ed4a.json` lists only scoped `xcodebuild`/build-for-testing commands and `interactiveChecks: []`.

summary: Reviewed feature metadata, worker handoff/transcript skeleton, skill requirements, and commit `f64db49db62f71fbce59c8f25d46f1e82cca6d1e`. While view-level error rendering coverage was added, Retry control presence/wiring assertions remain insufficiently strict, so the feature does not fully satisfy the required actionable-control test contract.
